# Sof Zman Shema

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Sof Zman Shma 3 Hours Before Chatzos

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning, calculated as 3 regular clock hours before chatzos hayom. This is generally known as part of the “Komarno” zmanim after [Rav Yitzchak Eizik of Komarno](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Komarno_(Hasidic_dynasty)#Rabbi_Yitzchak_Eisik_Safrin), a proponent of this calculation, though the documentation notes that it predates him.

3 regular clock hours, not shaos zmaniyos, before chatzos hayom.

This zman may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShma3HoursBeforeChatzos`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShma3HoursBeforeChatzos`

## Sof Zman Shma Alos 16 Point 1 To Sunset

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning based on the opinion that the day starts at alos 16.1° and ends at sea level sunset. This is the opinion of the [חידושי וכללות הרז״ה](https://hebrewbooks.org/40357) and the [מנורה הטהורה](https://hebrewbooks.org/14799) as mentioned by Yisrael Vehazmanim [vol. 1, sec. 7, ch. 3 no. 16](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=9765&pgnum=81).

3 shaos zmaniyos are calculated from a day that runs from alos 16.1° to sea level sunset, and added to alos.

Based on this calculation, chatzos will not be at midday.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaAlos16Point1ToSunset`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaAlos16Point1ToSunset`

## Sof Zman Shma Alos 16 Point 1 To Tzais Geonim 7 Point 083 Degrees

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning based on the opinion that the day starts at alos 16.1° and ends at tzais 7.083°.

3 shaos zmaniyos are calculated from a day that runs from alos 16.1° to tzais 7.083°, and added to alos 16.1°.

Based on this calculation, chatzos will not be at midday.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaAlos16Point1ToTzaisGeonim7Point083Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaAlos16Point1ToTzaisGeonim7Point083Degrees`

## Sof Zman Shma Ateret Torah

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning based on the calculation of Chacham Yosef Harari-Raful of Yeshivat Ateret Torah.

The day starts 1/10th of the day before sunrise and is usually calculated as ending 40 minutes after sunset. Shaos zmaniyos are calculated based on this day, and 3 shaos zmaniyos are added to alos.

The end of the day is configurable to an offset other than 40 minutes after sunset.

Based on this calculation, chatzos will not be at midday.

This zman may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaAteretTorah`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaAteretTorah`

## Sof Zman Shma Baal Hatanya

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the Baal Hatanya.

3 shaos zmaniyos after netz amiti, sunrise, based on the Baal Hatanya’s opinion that the day is calculated from sunrise to sunset.

This zman may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaBaalHatanya`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaBaalHatanya`

## Sof Zman Shma GRASunrise To Fixed Local Chatzos

[Rav Moshe Feinstein’s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein) opinion for the latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon), where the day is calculated from sunrise to sunset, but using only the first half of the day.

The half-day starts at sunrise and ends at fixed local chatzos. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after sunrise, which is half of this half-day.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaGRASunriseToFixedLocalChatzos`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaGRASunriseToFixedLocalChatzos`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 120 Minutes

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the opinion of the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), based on alos being 120 minutes, or 1/6th of the day, before sunrise. The documentation describes this as an extremely early zman and very much a chumra.

3 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, based on the MGA opinion that the day runs from dawn 120 minutes before sunrise to nightfall 120 minutes after sunset.

This zman may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA120Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA120Minutes`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 16 Point 1 Degrees

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the opinion of the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), based on alos being when the sun is 16.1° below the horizon before sunrise.

3 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, based on the MGA opinion that the day runs from dawn to nightfall, with both calculated as 16.1° below sunrise or sunset.

This zman may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA16Point1Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA16Point1Degrees`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 16 Point 1 Degrees To Fixed Local Chatzos

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to [Rav Moshe Feinstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein), following the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner) view that the day is counted from dawn to nightfall, but using only the first half of the day.

The half-day begins at alos, defined as when the sun is 16.1° below the horizon, and ends at fixed local chatzos. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after that alos, which is half of this half-day.

This zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA16Point1DegreesToFixedLocalChatzos`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA16Point1DegreesToFixedLocalChatzos`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 18 Degrees

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), with the day counted from dawn to nightfall.

Alos is defined as when the sun is 18° below the horizon before sunrise, and nightfall is when the sun is 18° below the horizon after sunset. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using solar hours based on this 18° dawn-to-nightfall day.

This zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA18Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA18Degrees`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 18 Degrees To Fixed Local Chatzos

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to [Rav Moshe Feinstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein), following the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner) view that the day is counted from dawn to nightfall, but using only the first half of the day.

The half-day begins at alos, defined as when the sun is 18° below the horizon, and ends at fixed local chatzos. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after alos, which is half of this half-day.

This zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 19 Point 8 Degrees

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), with the day counted from dawn to nightfall.

Alos is defined as when the sun is 19.8° below the horizon before sunrise, and nightfall is when the sun is 19.8° below the horizon after sunset. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using solar hours based on this 19.8° dawn-to-nightfall day.

This zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 72 Minutes To Fixed Local Chatzos

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to [Rav Moshe Feinstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein), following the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner) view that the day is counted from dawn to nightfall, but using only the first half of the day.

The half-day begins at alos, defined as 72 minutes before sunrise, and ends at fixed local chatzos. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after this alos, which is half of this half-day.

This zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA72MinutesToFixedLocalChatzos`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA72MinutesToFixedLocalChatzos`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 72 Minutes Zmanis

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), with the day counted from dawn to nightfall.

Alos is 72 minutes zmaniyos, or 1/10 of the day, before sea level sunrise, and nightfall is 72 minutes zmaniyos after sea level sunset. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using solar hours based on this day.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA72MinutesZmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA72MinutesZmanis`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 90 Minutes

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), with the day counted from dawn to nightfall.

Alos is 90 minutes before sunrise, and nightfall is 90 minutes after sunset. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using solar hours based on this 90-minute dawn-to-nightfall day.

This zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA90Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA90Minutes`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 90 Minutes To Fixed Local Chatzos

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to [Rav Moshe Feinstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein), following the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner) view that the day is counted from dawn to nightfall, but using only the first half of the day.

The half-day begins at alos, defined as 90 minutes before sunrise, and ends at fixed local chatzos. Sof zman Shema is 3 shaos zmaniyos after this alos, which is half of this half-day.

This zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA90MinutesToFixedLocalChatzos`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA90MinutesToFixedLocalChatzos`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 90 Minutes Zmanis

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), using a day that begins at dawn 90 proportional minutes before sunrise and ends at nightfall 90 proportional minutes after sunset.

It is calculated as 3 proportional hours after dawn, where dawn is 90 proportional minutes before sunrise and nightfall is 90 proportional minutes after sunset.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA90MinutesZmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaMGA90MinutesZmanis`

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), using a day that begins at dawn 96 minutes before sunrise and ends at nightfall 96 minutes after sunset.

It is calculated as 3 proportional hours after dawn, where dawn is 96 minutes before sunrise and nightfall is 96 minutes after sunset.

## Sof Zman Shma GRA

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon).

It is calculated as 3 proportional hours after sunrise. The day is calculated from sunrise to sunset, or from sea-level sunrise to sea-level sunset, depending on the elevation setting.

This time depends on whether elevation is used.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaGRA`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getSofZmanShmaGRA`

## Sof Zman Shma MGA 72 Minutes

The latest time to recite Shema in the morning according to the [Magen Avraham (MGA)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avraham_Gombiner), using a day that begins at dawn 72 minutes before sunrise and ends at nightfall 72 minutes after sunset.

It is calculated as 3 proportional hours after dawn, where dawn is 72 minutes before sunrise and nightfall is 72 minutes after sunset.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
